exemplar.title: Roots to Branches: The Story of My Family on Kikungshan

detailedExemplarData.title: Roots to Branches: The Story of My Family on Kikungshan

strengths: Excellent use of diverse primary and secondary sources, showing strong information-literacy skills
Effective demonstration of affective self-management in seeking critical feedback
Skillful adaptation of tone for different media, indicating audience awareness
Appropriate selection of digital tools (iMovie, After Effects), reflecting technical competence
weaknesses: Insufficient documentation of your digital source evaluation process (credibility checks, cross-referencing)
Lack of specificity around scheduling feedback sessions to optimize revision workflow
No concrete example of a revision altered by audience feedback to illustrate impact
Missing explicit skill-development goals for each software tool to show progressive mastery
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You display **robust ATL skill application**, particularly in research methods and self-management, and choose tools intelligently to enhance your product. To elevate this further, include **detailed evidence and procedural clarity**âhow you vetted sources, structured feedback loops, and defined milestones for digital-tool proficiency.

strengths: Establishment of a strong personal connection to the project, grounding the learning goal in authentic motivation
Clear conceptualization of a two-part product, demonstrating thoughtful scope and relevance
Articulate justification of functionality and aesthetic considerations, showing awareness of product requirements
weaknesses: Absence of detailed, measurable success criteria to track progress (e.g., number of sources, narrative length)
Lack of target completion dates and specific resource assignments for each phase, weakening the planâs feasibility
No formal distinction between content and production research deliverables, leading to potential confusion in sequencing
Missing practical shot list and detailed editing milestones (e.g., first draft cut, B-roll integration) to guide the production process
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates **clear vision and personal investment**, with a well-conceived product framework and thoughtful criteria justification. However, the plan remains **too broad and lacks the granular detail**âmeasurable checkpoints, resource allocation, and structured sequencingâthat would ensure consistent momentum and allow you to monitor progress rigorously.

strengths: Thorough evaluation of product functionality, reflecting on reader comments and view-through data
Insightful research reflection, especially in resolving conflicting information
Clear articulation of personal growth in time management
Perceptive reflection on cultural empathy, indicating deep personal learning
Comprehensive assessment of audience reach, signaling awareness of impact
weaknesses: Lack of direct linkage between evidence (e.g., comments, data) and each success criterion
No drafted review questions for translators and reviewers to ensure systematic post-translation refinement
Failure to connect cultural-empathy insights to specific future ATL objectives
Absence of concrete feedback excerpts (reader emails, fair comments) to ground evaluations
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections are **thoughtful and wide-ranging**, showing genuine self-awareness and learning. To complete the reflective cycle, anchor your insights in **specific evidence and actionable strategies**, tying each observation back to criteria and setting clear goals for future development.

annotations.comment: Short-term goals are thorough; adding target completion dates and assigning specific resources will help maintain momentum and track precise progress.
Your filming strategy is clear; formalize a shot list covering key scenes (wide, medium, close-up) to ensure your video meets optimal visual variety.
Distinguish between content and production research deliverables to streamline your learning plan and ensure each research phase has clear outcomes.

Your learning goal outlines a clear investigation of family history, but success criteria are missing. Define measurable criteria (e.g., number of sources analyzed, narrative word count) to track your progress effectively.
You establish a strong personal connection to the project; to deepen this, add specific examples of prior research challenges that directly motivated your deeper investigation.
Your two-part product is well-conceived; consider adding a detailed resource list (interviewees, software, archival access) to strengthen feasibility and sequencing.
Your criteria justification explains functionality and aesthetics; align each strand with measurable checkpoints (e.g., draft deadlines, feedback rounds) to enhance your planâs precision.
Your timeline phases are clear; specify resources and deliverable dates for each phase so sequencing and required support are fully detailed.
Your editing process outline is sound; integrate specific editing milestones (e.g., first draft cut, B-roll integration) to improve sequencing and resource management.
Excellent use of diverse primary and secondary sources; document your digital source evaluation process (credibility checks, cross-referencing) to showcase rigorous information literacy.
Strong demonstration of affective self-management in seeking critical feedback; clarify how you scheduled feedback sessions to optimize your revision workflow.
You adapt tone effectively for different media; cite a specific revision where audience feedback directly influenced your narrative style to strengthen this reflection.
Good tool selection for video editing and animation; outline explicit skill-development goals for each software (iMovie, After Effects) to demonstrate effective application of digital skills.
Your functionality evaluation is detailed; link specific evidence (reader comments, view-through data) to each success criterion for a more robust reflection.
Reflecting on your editing process is insightful; next time, draft specific review questions for translators and reviewers to ensure comprehensive post-translation refinement.
Your research reflection is strong; add commentary on how you resolved conflicting information and how that influenced your final narrative.
You capture personal growth in time management well; define specific strategies (e.g., Pomodoro intervals, task breakdown) to apply in future large-scale projects.
Your reflection on cultural empathy is perceptive; connect this insight to future ATL objectives to complete the reflective cycle with actionable goals.
Your assessment of reaching target audiences is thorough; include specific feedback excerpts (e.g., reader emails, fair comments) to ground your self-evaluation in concrete evidence.
